Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: modern North American fine arts styles and movements [300108689], modern North American [300018196], modern American [300018115], The Americas [300016617], styles, periods, and cultures by region [300111079], Styles and Periods (hierarchy name) [300015646], Styles and Periods Facet [300264088]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: A style inspired by American Impressionists and unique to Pennsylvania and surrounding areas.The Pennsylvania Impressionists painted the natural beauty of the local countryside around the turn of the century. These artists focused less on optics and ephemeral light effects than their French counterparts but utilized similar brushwork to express an immediacy and expressive urgency in their depictions of nature. The Pennsylvania Impressionist artists were united by their connections to the vibrant New Hope area of Pennsylvania. In the early 20th century artists of diverse backgrounds were attracted to Bucks County for its rural landscape and proximity to Philadelphia. Many artists studied and taught with one another at the Pennsylvania Academy of the Fine Arts and the Philadelphia School of Design for Women (now the Moore College of Art and Design).

Source.Source_ID: 2000098343/Westmoreland Museum of American Art. An American Tradition: The Pennsylvania Impressionists.

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Een stijl die is geïnspireerd door Amerikaanse impressionisten en uniek is voor Pennsylvania en omringende gebieden. De Pennsylvania-impressionisten beeldden eind 19de en begin 20ste eeuw in hun schilderijen het natuurschoon van het platteland uit. De schilders werkten minder met optische en lichteffecten dan de Franse impressionisten, maar gebruikten wel een vergelijkbare penseelvoering om directheid en expressieve urgentie uit te drukken in hun natuurschilderingen. De Pennsylvania-impressionisten waren allen verbonden met het bruisende gebied bij New Hope in Pennsylvania. In het begin van de 20ste eeuw werden kunstenaars met verschillende achtergronden aangetrokken door Bucks County, vanwege het landelijke karakter en de nabijheid van Philadelphia. Veel kunstenaars studeerden en doceerden samen aan de Pennsylvania Academy of the Fine Arts en de Philadelphia School of Design for Women (nu het Moore College of Art and Design).

Source.Source_ID: 2000098342/Pennsylvania Impressionism and Landscape: From the Collection of Woodmere Art Museum and the Promised Gift of Dr. Dorothy J. del Bueno. Woodmere Art Museum, 2011.
2000098343/Westmoreland Museum of American Art. An American Tradition: The Pennsylvania Impressionists.
